skill.name: plotly
skill.description: Create interactive Plotly charts for exploratory analysis, dashboards, presentations, and web-embeddable visualizations.
skill.summary: Build interactive Python visualizations with Plotly Express and Graph Objects.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns. Review shows documentation false positives from markdown fences, Plotly API names, official URLs, and export examples. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden executable code, or C2 behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick locations are markdown fences and install examples. They are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown fences and package installation examples. I found no skill code that executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Static blocker findings are semantic false positives
low_findings.description: C2 keyword, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary Plotly text. No cryptography, reconnaissance command, or C2 behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is visualization documentation and Plotly API guidance. The scanner matched substrings inside benign terms.

low_findings.title: Documentation includes filesystem export examples
low_findings.description: The skill teaches Plotly export with write_html, write_image, and a basic Python file write. User-chosen paths could overwrite local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit chart export examples. I found no secret collection or unauthorized file access.

low_findings.title: External links and service promotion are present
low_findings.description: The skill links to Plotly resources and suggests K-Dense Web for complex workflows. Publishers should disclose this external-service promotion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external links are visible in documentation. I found no hidden network request or data transfer logic.

content.value_statement: Data teams need charts that support hover details, zooming, and web sharing. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Plotly chart creation, styling, export, and dashboard patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Plotly Express versus Graph Objects.
Provides examples for scatter, line, bar, statistical, map, financial, 3D, and specialized charts.
Shows hover labels, rangesliders, animation frames, buttons, dropdowns, and Dash integration patterns.
Covers subplots, templates, color scales, axes, legends, annotations, and responsive layout settings.
Documents export workflows for interactive HTML and static PNG, PDF, SVG, JPEG, and WebP outputs.
Guides multi-chart dashboards with shared axes and mixed chart types.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install Plotly or run code automatically.
Interactive charts require a browser, notebook, Dash app, or compatible renderer.
Static image export requires Kaleido and may require Chrome or Chromium.
Very large datasets may need aggregation or WebGL chart types for smooth interaction.

use_cases.title: Explore datasets interactively
use_cases.description: Create hoverable charts, trendlines, distributions, heatmaps, and linked visual views during analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare dashboard visuals
use_cases.description: Build Plotly charts for reports, KPI reviews, and embedded dashboard prototypes.

use_cases.title: Share scientific visualizations
use_cases.description: Generate styled figures, 3D plots, and exportable HTML or static image outputs for presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic chart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple Plotly Express figure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Plotly Express scatter chart from my dataframe using temperature on x, yield on y, and treatment as color.

prompt_templates.title: Compare distributions
prompt_templates.prompt: Build Plotly charts that compare measurements across groups with box, violin, histogram, and hover details.

prompt_templates.title: Design a dashboard figure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a multi-panel report visual.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 2 by 2 Plotly subplot dashboard with a line chart, bar chart, heatmap, and distribution plot using shared styling.

prompt_templates.title: Export an interactive report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for web sharing and static export.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an interactive Plotly HTML chart and a static PNG version with responsive layout, custom hover text, and clean labels.

output_examples.input: Create a Plotly scatter chart with a trendline and treatment colors.
output: Interactive scatter chart plan using Plotly Express.
Temperature mapped to the x-axis and yield mapped to the y-axis.
Treatment groups shown with distinct colors and hover details.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard view for sales by month, region, and product.
output: Line chart for monthly trend, bar chart for regions, heatmap for product mix, and box plot for distribution.
Consistent template, titles, axis labels, and legend placement.

output_examples.input: Export my chart for a web report and a presentation slide.
output: Interactive HTML export recommendation for the web report.
Static image export recommendation for the slide deck.
Notes about Kaleido, browser rendering, and file size options.

best_practices: Use Plotly Express first when a dataframe and standard chart type are enough.
Switch to Graph Objects for custom traces, subplots, shapes, and specialized figures.
Choose HTML export for interactivity and static image export for slides or documents.
anti_patterns: Using Graph Objects for simple charts that Plotly Express can create clearly.
Exporting interactive HTML with CDN assets when offline use is required.
Plotting very large raw datasets without aggregation, sampling, or WebGL options.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create interactive Plotly charts, dashboard figures, styled layouts, and exportable visualization outputs.

faq.question: When should I use Plotly Express?
faq.answer: Use Plotly Express for quick charts from pandas dataframes with automatic labels, colors, legends, and defaults.

faq.question: When should I use Graph Objects?
faq.answer: Use Graph Objects when you need precise trace control, custom layouts, complex subplots, or specialized chart types.

faq.question: Can it make static images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation covers static export formats such as PNG, PDF, SVG, JPEG, and WebP.

faq.question: Does the skill run Plotly code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent must run any generated code in their environment.

faq.question: Is it suitable for dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers multi-panel figures, interactivity controls, responsive layouts, and basic Dash integration patterns.
